seoTitle: Record Invoice Payments in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Match and record customer invoice payments accurately in QuickBooks Online, including full and partial settlements.
intro: Closing out an open invoice correctly keeps your accounts receivable ledger accurate. This guide shows how to record a customer's invoice payment in QuickBooks Online, whether it's a full settlement or a partial payment.

steps.heading: Open Receive Payment
steps.body: Click + New, then select Receive Payment from the Customers column.

steps.heading: Select the customer
steps.body: Choose the client's profile to automatically populate all of their outstanding open invoices.

steps.heading: Set payment details
steps.body: Enter the payment date and select the method used, such as check, cash, or credit card.

steps.heading: Route the funds
steps.body: Use the Deposit to dropdown to send funds to your checking account or the Undeposited Funds holding matrix.

steps.heading: Apply and save
steps.body: Check the box next to the specific invoice being settled, adjust the amount for partial payments if needed, and click Save and Close.

faq.question: Can I record a partial payment on an invoice?
faq.answer: Yes, you can adjust the Payment value field to less than the full invoice amount, and QuickBooks will keep the remaining balance open.

faq.question: Where should payment funds be deposited?
faq.answer: You can route them directly to your checking account or hold them in Undeposited Funds until they're grouped into a bank deposit.

faq.question: How do I confirm an invoice is fully paid?
faq.answer: Check that the outstanding balance drops to zero after applying the payment, either on the invoice itself or the customer's ledger.

faq.question: Do I need to select a payment method?
faq.answer: Yes, selecting the correct method, like check or credit card, helps keep your records accurate for matching against bank feeds later.
